sourceMovie.title: Love, Simon
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Love, Simon (2018) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Greg Berlanti's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Spectacular Now
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Love, Simon clicked for you, The Spectacular Now (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Tall Girl
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Love, Simon clicked for you, Tall Girl (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Pride & Prejudice
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Love, Simon clicked for you, Pride & Prejudice (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Love, Simon?
faq.answer: Try The Spectacular Now, Tall Girl, Pride & Prejudice—each shares DNA with Love, Simon while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Love, Simon?
faq.answer: Love, Simon (2018) is best described as Comedy, Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Love, Simon worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
